pi_items.source: Verified in verifier.py and export_result.sh which both check this exact path.

metadata_value: basicTimeStep set to 256 (should be 8-64 for accurate robot simulation)
WorldInfo gravity set to 0.0 (should be ~9.81 for Earth simulation)
BLUE_PLAYER_1 robot Physics mass set to 0.0 (invalid - causes floating/no physics)
pi_items.source: Analyzed setup_task.sh and soccer.wbt. The script crashes and exits with code 1 at 'grep -q basicTimeStep' because Webots R2023b does not include this field natively in soccer.wbt. Even bypassing the crash, the regexes for Error 1 and 3 fail completely due to absent fields and non-matching node names.
Verified via verifier.py which strictly checks basicTimeStep <= 64, gravity >= 9.0, and mass > 0.1.
Verified via setup_task.sh which copies soccer.wbt from Webots demos.

pi_items.metadata_value: Senior Robotics Simulation Engineer / QA Engineer
pi_items.verified_value: Senior Robotics Simulation Engineer / QA Engineer

privileged_info_summary: The baseline Webots demo world (soccer.wbt) is utilized, but setup_task.sh contains a fatal flaw: it exits immediately because 'basicTimeStep' is absent from the R2023b soccer.wbt file. Consequently, none of the 3 claimed physics errors (basicTimeStep, gravity, mass=0) are actually injected. Furthermore, the verifier requires all robot masses to be > 0.1 kg, meaning an agent would have to artificially inflate the mass of existing robots (like the wheels which natively weigh 0.005 kg) to pass, rather than fixing a single injected 0.0 kg error.
